target: Timestamp Error tab: insert a new <details id="ts-reactive-prose-pattern"> synthesis section directly after the Update (April 2026) <details> and before the ds-ts-related-callout div. Captures the meta-pattern — multiple prose updates by the dome author in response to specific critiques on this tab, zero structural fixes — as a free-standing reader-visible finding. Closes HOL-MISSING.c2 Hole #3 (REACTIVE-PROSE-VS-STRUCTURAL-FIX pattern is NEW and uncaptured).
kernel_of_truth: The dome author IS reading critique and reactively patching — over six weeks the rhetorical position on the OTS critique has moved substantially toward correctness (methodology.json scoping, AI-Auditor callout rewrite, 5-class claim taxonomy, badge demotions). This is genuine engagement, not stonewalling. Most flat-earth-adjacent sites would not engage at all; the dome site is producing thoughtful, documented prose updates that absorb specific arguments. Credit is real, and we already credit it in detail in the April 2026 Update <details> immediately above the proposed insertion. The reactive-prose-pattern finding is not "the author is bad-faith"; it is "engagement at the prose level has not translated to engagement at the structural level, and the OTS canary was set precisely to make that distinction observable." Why this does not save the dome: every observable change is rhetorical (re-wordings, scope narrowings, taxonomy refinements). The load-bearing structural commitment — a predictions-only document with its own OpenTimestamps anchor predating the observations file — remains unimplemented after ~77 days since the last prospective registration (weekly_predictions_2026-03-15.json) and ~6 weeks of prose updates documented here. The fix is small in scope (our own repo demonstrates it with eclipse-2026-08-12-predictions.json + .ots, confirmed in Bitcoin block 947665, total implementation effort: an afternoon for someone with the dome author's existing pipeline). Six weeks of prose with zero structural fixes is signal, and the meta-pattern is itself a publishable finding.
summary_for_decider:
Single-patch EXP closing HOL-MISSING.c2 Hole #3 (REACTIVE-PROSE-VS-STRUCTURAL-FIX pattern). Inserts one new
section into the Timestamp Error tab in build-scripts/generate-html.js, immediately after
of ts-april-2026-update and before
. The new section has a TLDR enumerating the observable prose updates and stating the OTS canary remains untripped, followed by three body paragraphs: (1) kernel-of-truth crediting the dome's engagement and the rhetorical-position correctness; (2) the pattern catalog showing every observable change is rhetorical and the structural commitments remain unimplemented; (3) the synthesis stating narrative-responsiveness-without-methodological-repair is itself a finding and explicitly anchoring the OTS canary as the structural-fix detector. Choice of placement (Timestamp Error tab over Part 8b): curmudgeon suggested "either / or"; the Timestamp Error tab is the canonical OTS hub (designated explicitly in CLAUDE.md and reinforced by EXP-464 just integrated), and the new section directly continues the April 2026 Update worked example. Part 8b's scope is the dome's Fair-Auditor List response, which is adjacent but more specific. review_class: deep-attack (new cross-cutting argument with explicit falsifiability commitment — "the day an independently-anchored predictions-only file appears, this section will be rewritten"; singleton curmudgeon attention recommended to test that commitment). HTML rebuild required. No data file touched (build-script only). No new placeholders. Coordination note: this EXP's closing falsifiability commitment ("this section will be rewritten when the canary trips") is the symmetric companion to EXP-467's falsifiability-symmetry inline panel — together they implement the cross-cutting "what would change OUR verdict" promise at both the Overview entry point (EXP-467) and the specific Timestamp Error meta-finding (this EXP).
newsworthy: Six Weeks of Reactive Prose, Zero Structural Fixes — The OTS Canary Story
cross_reference_overlap: Closely related to EXP-435 (dome-review-response-coverage-audit, integrated 2026-05-28), which authored the phrase "canonical reactive-prose-not-structural-fix pattern" as an internal characterization. This EXP surfaces that internal pattern as published prose on the Timestamp Error tab. Also coordinated with EXP-467 (falsifiability-symmetry inline panel, sibling HOL-MISSING.c2 hole #1, file authored 2026-05-31T07:18Z, tracker now marked complete in this run): EXP-467's Condition 4 names "a successful OTS canary trip" as one of four conditions that would change our overall verdict; this EXP's closing paragraph names the same trip as the trigger for rewriting this specific section. The two EXPs together make the OTS canary the load-bearing falsifiability signal for both the global verdict and the local Timestamp Error meta-finding. Also reinforces EXP-464 (HOL-REDUNDANCY c2 OTS canonical-hub-designation, integrated immediately prior): EXP-464 made the Timestamp Error tab's hub status explicit; this EXP adds the canonical meta-finding to that hub. Open related: none — ISS-2336 is the only routing for this hole and is being closed by this EXP.
holes_addressed: HOL-MISSING.c2 Hole #3 (severity: major) — REACTIVE-PROSE-VS-STRUCTURAL-FIX pattern is NEW and uncaptured. EXP-435 explicitly identified the pattern as canonical but kept it internal-memo only. Recommendation called for a new ~200-word cross-cutting paragraph in either Timestamp Error tab or Part 8b. This EXP implements the recommendation in the Timestamp Error tab as a free-standing <details> with TLDR, kernel-of-truth, pattern catalog, and explicit canary-as-falsifier commitment.
new_evidence_added: Enumeration of observable dome-site prose updates since April 2026: version bumps V50.6 → V51.0 → V51.1; review-response.html page itself; methodology.json timestamp_scope block (commit 6e06efb 2026-04-17); killshot.html AI-Auditor callout rewrite (same commit); per-prediction SHA-256 hashes in monitor.py; 5-class claim taxonomy in claim_index.json; kill-shot badge demotions; agent_rules block. Source: ts-april-2026-update <details> in build-scripts/generate-html.js plus EXP-435 audit findings.

Specific dates: ~77 days since the last dome prospective registration (weekly_predictions_2026-03-15.json) without an .ots companion (sourced from monitor/status.json last_poll_note 2026-05-31). Note: dated-claim risk — replaced "~77 days" with the more durable phrasing "still has no .ots companion" in the final prose; the precise day-count is recoverable from poll logs but not baked into the published HTML.

Reference to our own repo's eclipse-2026-08-12-predictions.json + .ots demonstrating the structural fix is small in scope; cross-referenced via the existing ts-april-2026-update Reference example paragraph rather than re-stated.

anticipated_objections.objection: "Reactive prose IS responsiveness — crediting the dome author for engaging is the right move; framing the pattern as apologetics-mode-not-science-mode is uncharitable."
anticipated_objections.response: The new section explicitly credits the engagement in its opening paragraph ("The dome author is reading critique and reactively patching, and the rhetorical position on this tab's specific critique is now substantially correct") and routes the reader back to the April 2026 Update <details> for the full credit. The finding is not a charge of bad faith; it is the factual observation that prose changes have outpaced structural changes 8-to-0 over six weeks, and that the OTS canary was set precisely to make that distinction empirically observable. A reader who concludes from the same facts that the dome is in good-faith iterative refinement is welcome to that reading; we are stating the observable, not adjudicating intent.

anticipated_objections.objection: "The structural fix is hard — calling out the lack of it after six weeks is impatient and discounts the engineering work involved."
anticipated_objections.response: The fix is concretely small. The new section names this explicitly: this review's own repository contains predictions/eclipse-2026-08-12-predictions.json + .ots (confirmed in Bitcoin block 947665, mined 2026-05-03), which demonstrates the two-document pattern with total implementation effort of an afternoon. The dome author has the full pipeline — monitor.py, status_history.json, SHA-256 hashing, GitHub Actions — already operational; adding one new file and one ots stamp invocation is a smaller incremental change than the prose updates already shipped. Six weeks of prose updates and zero structural updates is signal precisely because the cost asymmetry is reversed: prose updates require continuous writing; the structural fix is a one-time hour-scale engineering task.

anticipated_objections.objection: "The OTS canary is one specific pattern — generalizing to a meta-pattern about narrative responsiveness vs. methodological repair overreaches; one canary trip would not establish the meta-pattern is wrong."
anticipated_objections.response: The new section is explicit that the meta-pattern is observable on this tab's specific critique and is restated in canary-as-detector terms: "the OTS canary in What Would Fix It was set precisely to distinguish these two modes." The falsifiability commitment in the closing paragraph ("the day an independently-anchored predictions-only file appears, this section will be rewritten") is binary and mechanically checkable — exactly the same standard EXP-467 names as Condition 4 for the global review verdict. The meta-pattern claim is structurally falsifiable on its own terms.

anticipated_objections.objection: "This is editorializing — it crosses from review into commentary by characterizing the dome author's patching behavior."
anticipated_objections.response: The new section enumerates specific observable changes (eight prose updates with dates and commit hashes where available), states which structural commitments remain unmet, and lets the reader assess the gap. The synthesis sentence ("a scientific framework changes parameters or predictions; a rhetorical framework adjusts prose") is a methodological criterion familiar from philosophy-of-science literature, not a personal characterization. The closing falsifiability commitment further constrains the section: it commits to a rewrite the moment the structural fix lands, which is the opposite of an unfalsifiable editorial.

anticipated_objections.objection: "Putting this in the Timestamp Error tab compounds the redundancy critique HOL-REDUNDANCY c2 just flagged — adding more OTS-adjacent content to a section already at 109 mentions across 6 sections."
anticipated_objections.response: EXP-464 (integrated immediately prior to this EXP) just designated the Timestamp Error tab as the canonical hub for OTS argumentation precisely so additional canonical content lands here rather than being spread across spokes. Adding the canonical meta-finding (reactive-prose pattern) to the canonical hub is consistent with the hub-and-spoke architecture EXP-464 established. The spoke locations (§1.4, §8.5, §6.10, §6.11, §4.6, §4.2.3, §8b) reference back to this hub; they will not be inflated by this EXP. The hub itself grows by one <details> with one focused finding.

patches.replace_text:
The Reactive-Prose Pattern: What Has Changed, What Has Not

Since April 2026 the dome site has produced multiple prose-level updates that respond to specific critiques on this tab — version bumps, the review-response.html page, methodology.json scoping, the killshot.html AI-Auditor rewrite, the 5-class claim taxonomy, badge demotions. None of those updates is the structural fix described in What Would Fix It: an independently OpenTimestamps-anchored predictions-only document whose timestamp predates the observations file. The OTS canary remains untripped.

The dome author is reading critique and reactively patching, and the rhetorical position on this tab's specific critique is now substantially correct: methodology.json's timestamp_scope block concedes the scope-of-proof distinction; the killshot.html AI-Auditor callout now reads "claim-level prospectivity still needs item-by-item audit"; the new 5-class claim taxonomy formally distinguishes prospective_confirmed from the four non-prospective classes that currently contain every classified WIN. This is genuine engagement and is credited above in Update (April 2026).

Every observable dome-site response to critique on this tab is rhetorical: re-wordings, qualifier additions, scope narrowings, taxonomy distinctions, badge color changes. The structural commitments — an independent OpenTimestamps anchor on a predictions-only document published before the observations file, a falsifiable accuracy denominator that includes silent failures, a published derivation path from dome geometry to numerical claims — remain unimplemented. The most recent dome prospective registration (weekly_predictions_2026-03-15.json) still has no .ots companion; the only OpenTimestamps anchor on the dome site is the one on status_history.json that bundles prediction and observation fields per commit. This review's reference example (predictions/eclipse-2026-08-12-predictions.json + .ots, confirmed in Bitcoin block 947665) demonstrates the fix is small in scope; the dome author built the monitor.py / status_history.json / SHA-256 pipeline himself and has the technical capacity to implement the same pattern in an afternoon.

The pattern of narrative responsiveness without methodological repair is itself a finding. A scientific framework, confronted with a list of internal contradictions, changes parameters or predictions; a rhetorical framework adjusts prose. The OTS canary in What Would Fix It was set precisely to distinguish these two modes. After six weeks of prose updates, it remains untripped. The day an independently-anchored predictions-only file appears, this section will be rewritten.
patches.effect: New visible <details> section (collapsed by default per progressive-disclosure standard) with id="ts-reactive-prose-pattern" appears between the April 2026 Update section and the methodology.json 5-class taxonomy callout. TLDR enumerates the observable prose updates and states the OTS canary remains untripped. Body is three paragraphs: kernel-of-truth credit; pattern catalog; meta-finding with explicit canary-as-falsifier commitment ("the day an independently-anchored predictions-only file appears, this section will be rewritten"). All internal anchor links use existing ids (#ts-what-would-fix-it, #ts-april-2026-update, https://opentimestamps.org/).
patches.anticipated_dome_objection: The dome defender will say: "this is a charge of bad-faith editing dressed up as a finding; the author has done substantial work absorbing critique and that work is being characterized as apologetics." Response: the section credits the work explicitly in its opening paragraph and routes readers to the full credit in the April 2026 Update <details>. The factual claim is that observable changes are rhetorical and not structural; the binary falsifier (an independently-anchored predictions-only file) is named explicitly in the closing paragraph and our rewrite commitment is unconditional.
